steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: The left side of the blank—”In many languages, including English, these consonants are”—is an independent clause awaiting its predicate. The right side—”in some dialects of Mandarin, however, four such consonants are relatively common”—is a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule
steps.content: Choice B correctly applies the rule: a semicolon separates two independent clauses. By placing a semicolon after “rare,” it joins the two clauses properly. Choices C and D violate this rule by using only commas to join the clauses when a conjunctive adverb is present (comma splice), while choice A creates nonsense by using “and” to link an adjective with a prepositional phrase. That matches choice B — B is correct.
